assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend has created a recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable.
The entities involved have different functional currencies, so the receivable/payable gives rise to foreign exchange gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item to be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the resulting exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: General intragroup exclusion
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 otherwise restricts hedged items in consolidated financial statements to exposures with external parties, so intragroup items are excluded unless a specific exception applies.

approaches.label: Forecast intragroup transaction hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately allows certain highly probable forecast intragroup transactions to qualify in consolidated financial statements, which defines the pre-recognition alternative to a recognised receivable.
